title: A Conserved C-Terminal Region in Gp71 of the Small Isometric-Head Phage LL-H and ORF474 of the Prolate-Head Phage JCL1032 Is Implicated in Specificity of Adsorption of Phage to Its Host, Lactobacillus delbrueckii
summary: The paper discusses several phages (LL-H, LL-S, JCL1032) and their host-range mutants (LL-H-a7, LL-H-a8, LL-H-a21, LL-H-a23, LL-H-a24, LL-H(Bes-1)) infecting Lactobacillus delbrueckii subsp. lactis ATCC 15808. The phages exhibit different adsorption specificities and mutations in tail protein genes affecting host range. Phage LL-H and LL-S are closely related, while JCL1032 is a prolate-head, possibly temperate phage.

phage LL-H.receptor: determined by the C-terminal portion of the protein encoded by g71
phage LL-H.structural_proteins: genes g17 (major tail protein), g71 (minor tail protein)
phage LL-H.growth_medium: MRS broth supplemented with 10 mM CaCl2

phage LL-H(Bes-1).receptor: determined by the C-terminal portion of the protein encoded by g71
phage LL-H(Bes-1).structural_proteins: genes g17 (major tail protein), g71 (minor tail protein)
phage LL-H(Bes-1).growth_medium: MRS broth supplemented with 10 mM CaCl2
